title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs normalized feed domain, content variants, and provenance from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for loss-aware normalized feed and entry model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Convert format-specific transport values into validated feed, entry, person, link, category, content, and provenance domain types.
Choose entry identity from stable Atom ID, RSS GUID semantics, canonical link, or scoped content fingerprint with explicit precedence.
Preserve plain text, escaped HTML, XHTML, external content, summary, and missing content as distinct bounded states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Transport Domain Separation · changed case · Review Feed Go Transpo…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 9828 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Convert format-specific transport values into validated feed, entry, person, link, category, content, and provenance domain types.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Emit field-level admitted, defaulted, dropped, conflicted, and unsupported evidence for each normalization run.
